description:
First half of F2 (performance), now under epic mutagen-xez (split out of mutagen-33b). Coverage output is consumed in exactly two places today (enumerator + JSON report) and never turned into a line->test mapping, so per-mutant test selection is impossible. Build the index.

File: lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (run_under_cover :186-201 does ONE ExUnit run over all modules; gather_covered_lines/analyse_module :439-507 analyse per SOURCE module aggregated over the whole run).

## FEASIBILITY GATE (review finding H4 -- resolve BEFORE writing index code)
Per-test-MODULE attribution is NOT available with today's machinery, and the ticket must front-load the design + a measurement:
- :cover accumulates counters GLOBALLY across one ExUnit run; analyse(mod,:coverage,:line) returns covered lines per SOURCE module aggregated over ALL tests -- there is no per-test-module breakdown.
- The CoverFacade exposes only start/stop/compile_beam/analyse -- NO :cover.reset. The ExUnitFacade exposes only configure/run -- NO per-module run filter.
- So attributing a line to a TEST module requires: reset cover counters + run that test module ALONE + analyse, repeated per module => ~N cover-instrumented ExUnit runs where today there is ONE. The "or capture per-module deltas" alternative is the same N-run serialization (you cannot subtract one module from a globally-accumulated counter).
- Up-front cost is therefore an honest Nx of the coverage phase. For few-site / small-suite runs the added (N-1) coverage runs can make the tool SLOWER than the full-suite-per-site path it replaces.

Deliverables of this gate, IN this ticket, before the index build:
1. Facade design for per-module attribution (add :cover.reset + a single-/filtered-module ExUnit run, or an equivalent isolation mechanism). Specify how it composes with BeamCache snapshot ordering (cover-compile must precede snapshot, beam_cache.ex:36-38).
2. A break-even measurement: on a realistic suite, N (modules) vs S (sites) -- show the O(S*N)->O(S*k) saving exceeds the +(N-1) coverage-run cost. If it does not for the target workload, STOP (the optimization is not worth building).

## Approach (per-test-MODULE granularity -- the cheap, high-value cut, IF the gate passes)
Result shape: %{scope_file => %{line => MapSet.t(test_module)}}. Per-test precision (one cover run per test) is a later refinement.

## Done
- Feasibility gate above resolved: facade design landed + break-even measurement recorded in this ticket.
- CoverageRunner produces the line->{test module} index for the scope under test.
- Empty/missing entries are representable (drives the safe fallback in F2b).
- Unit test pins the index shape against a small fixture with two test modules touching disjoint lines.
title: F2a: CoverageRunner emits a line -> {test module} index
